redFlags.evidence: Stolen twice from the same museum: once in 1977 (recovered a decade later in Kuwait) and again on 21 August 2010, cut from its frame in daylight. Not recovered.
Egyptian officials reported that at the time of the 2010 theft only a small fraction of the museum's cameras were operational and the gallery alarms were not working.
Reported to INTERPOL's Stolen Works of Art database; any appearance on the market is a criminal matter, not a due-diligence question.

repatriation.note: An unsolved theft from a state museum, not a restitution claim. Egyptian ownership is undisputed.

journey.event: Painted during Van Gogh's Paris period
Acquired by the collector Mohamed Mahmoud Khalil
Passes to the Egyptian state; hangs in the Khalil Museum
Recovered in Kuwait and returned to the museum
Stolen a second time; cut from its frame

sources.title: INTERPOL Stolen Works of Art Database

identifyingRegisters.name: INTERPOL Stolen Works of Art
identifyingRegisters.requires: a theft reported to and recorded by a member-country police force

identifyingRegisters.name: INTERPOL ID-Art
identifyingRegisters.requires: the same police report as the Stolen Works of Art database

identifyingRegisters.name: FBI National Stolen Art File
identifyingRegisters.requires: a law-enforcement submission and a value threshold

identifyingRegisters.name: Art Loss Register
identifyingRegisters.requires: a registered loss, usually by an owner or insurer

weakRegisters.name: Wikidata
weakRegisters.why: Coverage follows scholarly and press attention rather than rule, so its silence carries little information.

blindRegisters.name: Carabinieri TPC
blindRegisters.why: Geographically out of scope for egypt — requires an Italian nexus — the archive is national.

blindRegisters.name: Lost Art Database
blindRegisters.why: Holds nazi-era losses; requires a loss connected to Nazi persecution, 1933–45.

blindRegisters.name: Getty Provenance Index
blindRegisters.why: Geographically out of scope for egypt — requires passage through the European or American art market.

blindRegisters.name: ICOM Red Lists
blindRegisters.why: Holds archaeological, colonial losses; requires membership of an at-risk object category from a listed region.

coverage.note: 4 of 9 registers could systematically name this object. Silence here is comparatively informative: records would be expected to exist.
coverage.comparability: This score is comparable only to other well covered objects. Comparison against a structurally uncovered object will understate how little was checked there.
